title: Speaking: Time and Age
learningGoal: Engage in a conversation stating your age, asking for the time, and mentioning the day of the week.
instructions: In this roleplay, you are talking to a young relative. You will state your age, ask them for the time, and tell them what day it is today.
speakingPrompt: Speak clearly and pay attention to the gender marker 'ka' or 'ki' when stating your age.
scenario: A casual conversation with a young family member.
role: Yourself (Assume you are 10 years old for this exercise)

subTasks.prompt: Your cousin asks: 'Aap kitne saal ke hain?' (How old are you?). Reply: 'I am 10 years old.' (Use 'ka hoon' if you are male, 'ki hoon' if female. The sample answer uses 'ka hoon').
Now ask your cousin: 'What time is it?'
Your cousin replies: 'Chaar baje hain. Kal kya hai?' (It is 4 o'clock. What is tomorrow?). Tell them: 'Tomorrow is Thursday.'
Now tell your cousin: 'Today is Wednesday.'

acceptableParaphrases: Using 'ki hoon' for female speakers is perfectly correct.
Using real numbers for your age is also acceptable.

criteria.name: Time Question Formation
criteria.description: Must use 'Kitne' (plural) and 'hain' (plural) for asking the time.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Main das saal ka hoon. Kitne baje hain? Kal guruvaar hai. Aaj budhvaar hai.

feedbackRules.onPass: Superb! You can naturally discuss the time, date, and age.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the question for time. It must be 'Kitne baje hain?'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Pay attention to your pronunciation of 'das' and 'baje'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: मैं दस साल का हूँ। कितने बजे हैं? कल गुरुवार है। आज बुधवार है।

hintText: Speak smoothly. 'Kitne baje hain' should sound like one phrase: Kitne-baje-hain.
commonMistakes: Saying 'Kitna baje hai' (using the singular instead of plural).
